blocks.content: I used to think I was a good person.

I don't think about that anymore. Good and bad are words people use when they haven't been pushed far enough to understand that the line between them is not a line at all — it's a distance. A distance that gets shorter the longer you carry something alone.

By the time this weekend is over, it will be finished. The weight I've been carrying will have somewhere to go. And he will finally understand — in whatever way a person understands something at the very end — what it cost me. What he cost me.
I've said his name so many times in my head, in the dark, going over everything. I've rehearsed this.
Today is the day I finally do it.
